children.text: The next time someone asks - “What’s your gut telling you?” - you might want to listen closely to the answer/to the voice inside your belly.

Why? Because the gut and brain can “talk” to each other through nerves, and there is a growing body of evidence that this bidirectional, back-and-forth communication between your digestive system and your central nervous system can have a major impact on the health of each other.

It might seem weird that your gut has the ability to influence your mental state-of-mind, but if you’ve ever felt “butterflies” or had a “nervous stomach” or gotten a “gut feeling” then you already know there’s a link.

This connection, known as the gut-brain axis, makes some scientific sense too. Research shows there are more nerve cells in your gut than anywhere else in the human body (besides the brain) and more information passes between the brain and digestive system than any other system in the body.
Exploring the Link Between Gut Health and Mental Health

The gut-brain axis refers to the two-way communication network that links the gastrointestinal (GI) system to the brain and central nervous system, and this connection has garnered significant attention in recent years.

Studies have shown that people with depression (and other mental health diagnoses) often have altered gut biomes compared to healthy individuals, and for years, scientists postulated that mental health issues came first and GI problems came next.

But new data and observational research are turning this hypothesis on its head. And now scientists believe that an unhealthy gut can actually contribute to mental health issues and not just be the effect of them.

Research has also demonstrated that probiotics – live microorganisms that can confer health benefits – can have a positive impact on mental health and has been associated with improved cognitive function and reduced depression and anxiety in some people.
The gut-brain connection involves other mechanisms besides the digestive system and nervous system and also includes the immune system, endocrine system, and hormonal pathways.
There are several key players at the heart of this gut-brain relationship:
Enteric Nervous System

The enteric nervous system describes the network of nerves that operate within the GI tract and control digestion. With hundreds of millions of neurons, it’s the most complex ‘nervous system’ outside of the brain, which has led some scientists to refer to it as a ‘second brain’.

This system is part of the autonomic nervous system, which governs the automatic/involuntary functions of your internal organs. This means, while you’re busy doing other things – sleeping, working, playing – the enteric nervous system is continuously gathering and processing information about your GI system and responding without much input from the brain.
The Vagus Nerve

The vagus nerve is a major nerve that runs from the brainstem to the abdomen, and it plays a crucial role in transmitting signals from the enteric nervous system to the brain. This nerve shares sensory information about the gut and receives motor signals from the brain, which allows it to mount a rapid response to chemical changes and the presence of food.
Gut Microbiome

Another key player in the gut-brain connection is the gut microbiome – a complex community of microorganisms, like bacteria, viruses, and fungi, that reside in the digestive tract. These microbes help produce neurotransmitters that convey messages between the gut and brain, and they actively participate in numerous physiological functions, including digestion and immune function.

There is significant overlap between people who have functional gastrointestinal disorders, like IBS (irritable bowel syndrome), and mental health problems, like anxiety, and recent studies show that the gut microbiome likely plays a role in this observation.
Experiencing digestive issues? Join Tia's membership and schedule an appointment to get checked at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.
How Gut Health Influences Mental Health
The relationship between gut health and mental health is complicated, and researchers are just starting to better understand the connection. Here are a few of the mechanisms involved:
Neurotransmitter Production:

Many of the neurotransmitters critical for mood regulation, such as serotonin, dopamine, and gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), are produced in the gut. In fact, approximately 90% of the body's serotonin - a neurotransmitter associated with feelings of well-being and happiness - is produced in the gut.
Inflammation and the Immune System:

Chronic inflammation is increasingly recognized as a contributing factor to various mental health conditions, including depression and anxiety. The gut microbiome helps regulate the body's immune response, and an imbalance in gut bacteria can lead to increased inflammation, impaired brain function, and the development of mental health disorders.
The Gut Barrier and Leaky Gut Syndrome:

The gut lining serves as a barrier that prevents harmful substances, such as toxins and pathogens, from entering the bloodstream. When this barrier is compromised - a condition often referred to as "leaky gut" - it can allow these harmful substances to enter the bloodstream. This process triggers an immune response and inflammation, which can lead to cognitive decline and mental health issues.
Stress Response and the HPA Axis:

The hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis is a central part of the body's stress response system. Research suggests that the gut microbiome can influence the activity of the HPA axis and affect how the body responds to stress. Unhealthy amounts of stress can cause inflammation and impaired immunity which, in turn, can cause mental health problems.
Steps to Support Gut and Mental Health

Considering the strong connection between gut health and mental health, it pays to have a strategy for supporting and improving your brain and belly health. Experts continue to explore the best ways to nurture the gut-brain connection, but for now, their best advice involves two primary considerations: improving your diet and reducing your stress.
Improving Your Diet

A diet rich in fiber, fruit, vegetables, and fermented food can promote a healthier gut biome. Yogurt, kefir, and kimchi contain gut-friendly probiotics while garlic, onions, and bananas have prebiotics that help feed healthy gut bacteria.
Antioxidants and anti-inflammatory foods, which are found in whole foods, like fruits and vegetables, fight harmful free radicals and can help prevent inflammation.
Limiting the intake of processed food, sugar, and alcohol can help too. These substances can disrupt the gut biome and cause an inflammatory response.
Reducing Stress
Chronic stress has a major impact on gut and brain health, so finding effective ways to manage stress is crucial to maintaining a strong gut-brain connection.
Mindfulness practices, like meditation and yoga, deep breathing exercises, regular exercise, and better sleep are all great ways to reduce stress and improve your gut and mental health.

Try therapy. Cognitive behavioral therapy, gut-directed relaxation training, and biofeedback are all wonderful ways to reduce stress and improve gut health, mental health, and overall health and wellbeing.

The gut-brain axis is a super highway of nerves and chemical messengers that connect our digestive system and central nervous system, and this connection underscores the importance of taking a holistic approach to health and wellbeing. While science continues to explore this complex relationship, we can nurture our gut biomes with lifestyle changes, like eating a healthy diet and reducing stress levels, and improve our gut health, brain health, and overall health and wellbeing in the process.
Need gut health support? Join Tia's membership and schedule an appointment to get checked at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

children.text: Consider acupuncture if you're:
Looking to support your hormones or hormone-related issues including PCOS, menopause, perimenopause, or cramping
Seeking relief from stress and/or support for anxiety or mood imbalances
Exploring alternatives for pain management for migraines, chronic pain, muscle soreness, or after a procedure like IUD insertion
Trying to conceive or seeking support on your pregnancy journey
Trust the science, the benefits are real
More than 2 out of 3 Tia members see improvement in their symptoms*

The intent of acupuncture is to bring the body back into a state of homeostasis and ignite the body’s innate ability to heal–and that can take some time. It often takes up to 6 treatments within a 3 month window to see significant results.
*Based on self reported data from N=120 Tia acupuncture patients who have had 6 or more appointments in the last 3 months.
What to expect when you try Acupuncture at Tia
The Environment
Relax and let everything melt away. Acupuncture at Tia is done in a semi-private room surrounded by curtains and supported by calming sounds and mood lighting.
The Appointment

Your 60-minute session is divided into two parts: You'll spend the first 20-minutes with your provider discussing your symptoms, goals and progress. Once you’ve aligned, your provider will get into the treatment. You'll spend the remaining 40-minutes in a deep state of relaxation, while the needles do their work.
Check our price list.
Find Acupuncture Services Near You
Meet our Acupuncturists

When you come to Tia for acupuncture, your acupuncturist will work in collaboration with your primary care providers, OB/GYN, and therapist. From two-part appointments like IUD insertion and acupuncture to offering holistic support for fertility, we believe in connected care that treats the whole you.

children.text: Annual Physical Exam

items: We focus on your preventative care for the year ahead.
We complete your comprehensive annual physical & well-woman exam.
We create your personalized preventative health plan, together.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Already a Tia member? Book here
What is this appointment?
First, we focus on your preventative health

This dedicated visit is the cornerstone of preventative care for women. We’ll review essential screenings, check in on your key health markers, and discuss your wellness goals—from nutrition to sleep—and build a proactive plan that helps you stay healthy.
Then, we complete your comprehensive exam

We combine your annual physical and well-woman exam into one convenient visit. This includes essential screenings like a Pap test (if you're due), breast exam, blood pressure check, and preventative blood work tailored to you.
Finally, we create your preventative plan together

More than just checking boxes, this visit should begin a conversation about what wellness means for you. We take your context and our knowledge to make a preventative care plan tailored to your unique needs and goals.
Who should have an Annual Physical Exam?
Everyone should have an Annual Physical Exam every year,

even if you are not due for a Pap test or don’t want a breast exam. These exams are focused on your holistic health and can be especially important if you’re managing a chronic condition, have notable family health history, or are moving through a significant life transition like planning for pregnancy, postpartum, or menopause.
What’s included in an Annual Physical Exam
A Preventative Health Discussion:
A focused conversation about your preventative health picture, including your lifestyle, goals, and any questions you have about staying healthy.
Physical Exam:
We’ll cover the essentials, including checking your vitals like blood pressure and a standard physical assessment.
Well-Woman Exam:
This includes a clinical breast exam and if you’re due, we'll perform a Pap test and HPV screening.
HPV Self-Collection Option:
For eligible patients who are unable to or uncomfortable with a traditional pelvic exam, we offer a self-collection option for HPV screening that you can do yourself, without a speculum.
Lab Work:
Your provider will order routine, preventative blood work based on your personal health risks and goals.
Personalized Care Plan:
We’ll wrap up by creating a preventative Care Plan that’s tailored to you, connecting this visit to your broader health journey.
Identify issues before they fester:
We work with you during this visit to identify health concerns and connect you to the right care to continue to work on those concerns going forward.
How an Annual Physical Exam can help

This check up is your dedicated time to feel empowered and in control of your health journey. It helps you stay on top of crucial preventative health screenings and get a clearer picture of your health with routine blood work. By partnering with your provider, you can better understand and manage your personal health risks, creating a proactive plan for prevention and long-term wellness
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?

Yes, most insurance plans cover one preventative exam per year at 100%. If it has been at least 12 months since your last annual and you stick to preventative topics, your insurance should cover the visit.
What if I discuss other health issues?

Your Annual Physical Exam is designed for preventative care. If you bring up new or specific health concerns (like a rash or headaches), your insurance provider may bill that portion of the visit as a regular office visit. This does not mean that office visit is not covered but depending on your insurance plan, it could result in a copay or co-insurance. To take advantage of your preventative health benefit, we recommend discussing other health concerns in a separate visit.
Our team of experts
Alternatives to prescription medication, emotional wellness

She leverages a holistic approach that marries traditional and alternative medicine to optimize patient health, striving to give all patients the tools to improve emotional as well as physical health.

At Tia, we offer comprehensive, holistic support from compassionate primary care providers, gynecologists, mental health specialists, and wellness practitioners who are here for your whole health.

Whether you prefer in-person visits or virtual appointments, our team works together to support your mental and physical health—across your whole life.

modules.title: Where to get an Annual Physical Exam

children.text: At Tia, there’s no difference—we see them as one and the same! We call the appointment an "Annual Preventative Exam," and during that visit, we can provide both your annual primary care physical and your well-woman exam at the same time. It’s all part of our approach to care for your whole self in one comprehensive visit.

faqs.question: What is the difference between an annual exam and a preventative exam?

children.text: At Tia, your labs are handled and billed separately through our third-party partner, Quest Diagnostics. While your preventative visit is typically covered by insurance, coverage for specific lab tests can vary between insurance plans. If you have any questions about the cost or billing for your bloodwork, it’s best to contact Quest or your insurance provider directly.

faqs.question: Does insurance cover bloodwork?

children.text: Absolutely! It is important to have an Annual Preventative Exam every year, even if you do not need a pap test. Your annual exam is about so much more than a single test; it’s a crucial time to connect with your provider, review your overall physical and mental health, and discuss other important screenings.

faqs.question: Do I need an annual exam every year, even if I'm not due for a Pap?

children.text: Your annual exam is designed for preventative care, which is typically covered 100% by insurance. If you bring up a new or ongoing health issue, your insurance may consider that part of the visit a separate "office visit," which could result in a copay or co-insurance. This does not mean that office visit is not covered but depending on your insurance plan, it could result in a copay or co-insurance. To take advantage of your preventative health benefit, we recommend discussing other health concerns in a separate visit.

faqs.question: What happens if I discuss a new health problem during my visit?

children.text: Yes, you can get your blood drawn onsite at our Tia clinics!

Your Tia provider will order the necessary labs for you. After your blood is drawn at our clinic, we send the sample to our lab partner, Quest, for processing. You’ll receive a separate bill directly from Quest for any lab services.

faqs.question: Can I get my bloodwork done at Tia?

children.text: It depends. At Tia, we typically code this examination as an annual gynecologic exam, but since we also do primary care, we can convert this to an annual primary care exam (assuming you have not visited both a gynecologist and a primary care clinician already for your annual exam). Please notify us when booking the appointment so that we can adjust your care accordingly.
Since individual insurance policies do vary quite a bit, you may want to consult with your insurance plan first before booking this appointment to be certain.

faqs.question: Can I book this with Tia if I have already had an annual exam with another provider?

children.text: At Tia, we believe your care should be a conversation about your personal wellness goals. Whether a specific lab is right for you depends on your situation, but we can discuss it during your annual preventative exam if it's relevant to your preventative care.

Please note that all billing for lab work goes through a 3rd party, Quest, who bills your insurance directly for all blood work. If you have questions about lab billing, please contact Quest or your insurance company.

faqs.question: I want to get lab X done. Is that included in my annual preventative exam at Tia?

children.text: These are different! An annual preventative exam is a standard yearly checkup focused on general health, disease prevention, and early detection. A longevity assessment is a much more in-depth and proactive approach that uses advanced diagnostics to create a personalized plan for optimizing your health and extending your "healthspan." You can read more about our Longevity care here.

faqs.question: Is an Annual Exam the same thing as a Longevity Assessment?

children.text: As women enter their 40s, many experience hormonal changes that can affect various aspects of their health. While this time can be full of vitality, it also marks a period when preventive health screenings become increasingly important. These screenings help detect early signs of conditions like perimenopause, breast cancer, and osteoporosis—conditions that can significantly impact a woman's quality of life as she ages. In this post, we'll cover the most important preventive health screenings for women in their 40s and beyond.
Cervical Cancer Screening

Cervical cancer screening is an essential part of preventive care, even for women in their 40s. According to the American Cancer Society, women between the ages of 40-65 have three options for cervical cancer screening:
Pap test
every 3 years
Pap test and HPV test (co-testing) every 5 years
HPV testing alone every 5 years
Once you reach age 65, you may be able to stop cervical cancer screening if:
You’ve had three consecutive negative Pap tests or two negative HPV tests in the past ten years.
You have no history of moderate or severe abnormal cervical cells or cervical cancer.
It’s important to talk to your healthcare provider about the best screening schedule based on your health history.
Breast Cancer Screening

Breast cancer is a significant health concern, but early detection through screening can improve outcomes. The American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) recommends that women with average risk begin regular breast cancer screenings with a mammogram starting at age 40.
You should continue getting mammograms every 1-2 years until age 75.
If you have a family history of breast cancer or genetic markers, you may need to start screening earlier or undergo additional imaging tests.
These screenings help detect abnormal changes or identify
breast concerns
early, which is critical for timely intervention.
Blood Pressure Screening

High blood pressure can quietly damage your heart, kidneys, and arteries over time. Women should get their blood pressure checked annually to ensure it stays within a healthy range. If your readings are elevated, your doctor can help you manage and monitor it before it leads to more serious conditions like heart disease or stroke.
Cholesterol Screening

Starting at age 45, women should get their cholesterol levels checked every 5 years. If you have certain risk factors—such as a history of diabetes, heart disease, or high blood pressure—you may need more frequent monitoring. Cholesterol levels are a key indicator of cardiovascular health, and early detection can help reduce your risk of heart disease.
Diabetes Screening

The American Diabetes Association recommends that women start diabetes screening every 3 years starting at age 45. If you are overweight or have additional risk factors like a family history of diabetes, screening may begin earlier. Detecting diabetes early allows you to manage the condition through lifestyle changes and medications, which can prevent complications like kidney disease, nerve damage, and cardiovascular issues.
Eye Exam

Your eye health is just as important as the rest of your body. Starting at age 40, women should get an eye exam every 2-4 years. From ages 55 to 64, eye exams should be conducted every 1-3 years. Early detection of eye conditions like cataracts, glaucoma, and macular degeneration can prevent vision loss. If you have a family history of eye conditions or are experiencing vision changes, your doctor may recommend more frequent exams.
Colon Cancer Screening

Colon cancer screening is vital for women starting at age 45, with the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force (USPSTF) recommending regular screenings until age 75. If you have a family history of colon cancer or conditions like inflammatory bowel disease, your provider may recommend earlier screenings.
There are various methods for screening, including:
Fecal occult blood tests (FOBT)
Flexible sigmoidoscopy
CT colonography
The gold standard for colon cancer screening is a colonoscopy, which should be repeated every 10 years or sooner, depending on your doctor’s recommendations.
Bone Density Screening

As women approach menopause, the risk of developing osteoporosis increases due to the decline in estrogen levels. The National Osteoporosis Foundation recommends that women begin bone density testing at age 65. If you have risk factors such as low body weight, family history of osteoporosis, or long-term steroid use, your doctor may recommend starting bone density testing earlier.
Early detection can help prevent fractures and manage bone health with lifestyle changes or medications.
Hearing Testing

Hearing loss can become more common with age, especially after 50. Women should undergo hearing screenings every 3 years starting at age 50. If you experience difficulty hearing, or if you have a family history of hearing loss, talk to your provider about more frequent testing. Early intervention can improve your quality of life by addressing issues before they worsen.
Lung Cancer Screening

Lung cancer screening is recommended for current or former smokers with a history of 20 pack-years (smoking one pack of cigarettes per day for 20 years) who are 50-80 years old. If you currently smoke or have quit within the past 15 years, you may qualify for annual lung cancer screenings with a low-dose CT scan. Early detection can improve survival rates for those at higher risk.

In your 40s, your body is undergoing natural changes, making preventive care more important than ever. This is the time to focus on regular screenings and health checks that can help detect potential risks early, before they become bigger issues. By staying proactive with your health, you're not only preventing future complications, but you're also gaining a deeper understanding of your body’s evolving needs, so you can make empowered choices about your health and wellness.
Now is the time to prioritize your health as you enter this new chapter. Whether it’s scheduling a
breast cancer screening
, getting a bone density scan, or having a conversation with your doctor about
perimenopause
, every check-up is an important step toward maintaining your overall well-being.
At Tia, we offer a full range of services—including
primary care
gynecological exams
mental health support
, and
skincare
— all in one convenient location.
Need a preventative exam? Join Tia and schedule an appointment at the clinic nearest you.
Located in New York, Scottsdale, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

metaInformation.twitterTitle: Preventive Health Screenings for Women in Their 40s and Beyond

children.text: In your 20s and 30s, health is often at its peak, but it’s still important to get regular check-ups and begin routine screenings that are recommended by your healthcare provider. Good health is often about good habits, and one healthy habit is to start getting recommended preventive screenings on a regular basis and doing it early and often.

What follows is a general guide to age-specific health screenings for women in their 20s and 30s. Professional medical organizations and government agencies don’t always agree on what screenings to get when, so this list isn’t a comprehensive rundown of screenings. However, it is a good starting point and a great way to start the conversation with your provider about the recommended preventive screenings for women that are best for you.
Cervical Cancer Screening
The
Pap smear

is the most-effective test we have for detecting cervical cancer at its earliest stage and pre-cancerous cellular changes that can lead to cervical cancer. An HPV test can detect high-risk types of human papilloma virus that are more likely to cause cervical cancer. At age 21, every woman should get a Pap smear every 3 years.

Women in their 30s have three options for testing. They can have a Pap test every 3 years or they can have a Pap test and HPV test (co-testing) every 5 years or they can have HPV testing alone every 5 years.
Breast Cancer Screening
Your provider should offer to perform an in-office
clinical breast exam

every 1-3 years during your 20s and 30s. Self-breast exams have become a controversial practice in recent years, and most providers don’t recommend them anymore. If you have an increased risk for breast cancer your provider may recommend a mammogram or additional testing before age 40. Risk factors for breast cancer include having a family history of breast cancer, ovarian cancer, or other inherited types of cancer and carrying a high-risk genetic marker for breast cancer.
Blood Pressure Screening

Your blood pressure should be taken at each wellness exam to check for early signs of hypertension, which is a leading cause of heart disease and stroke. Your provider may recommend earlier screening if you have diabetes, heart disease, kidney problems, obesity, or other health conditions.
Cholesterol Screening

It’s a good idea to get your cholesterol checked once in your 20s. This is especially true if you have risk factors for heart disease and stroke – a family history of heart disease, obesity, a sedentary lifestyle, and/or smoking.
Diabetes Screening

You should be screened for pre-diabetes and type 2 diabetes starting at age 35. Screening should be repeated every 3 years if you have certain risk factors, like obesity, high blood pressure, pre-diabetes, or a first-degree relative with diabetes.
STI Testing
If you’re sexually active, you should get tested for
STIs

every year from age 20-25. This can help rule out common infections, like chlamydia and gonorrhea, that often don’t present with symptoms – but can lead to big problems if left untreated. There is no strict guideline for continuing STI screening, but it’s always available if you have symptoms of an STI or fall into a high-risk category and your doctor recommends getting tested.
Skin Exam
You should start getting yearly full-body skin checks in your 30s.
Hepatitis C/HIV Testing
All adults should get a one-time test for hepatitis C and HIV.
General Health Screenings

Your height, weight, and BMI (body mass index) should be measured at every wellness exam, and there should always be a discussion about diet/exercise, alcohol/tobacco use, medications/interactions, and safety concerns, like intimate partner abuse. Your provider should also assess for mental health issues, like anxiety and depression.
Why Preventive Health Screenings is Important

In your 20s and 30s, your body is still at a stage where prevention and early detection can make a significant impact on your long-term health. By staying ahead of potential health risks with routine screenings, you’re setting yourself up for a healthier future. These early check-ups are not only about catching problems early but also about understanding your body and its unique needs, so you can make informed decisions about your lifestyle and wellness habits.
Now is the time to prioritize your health

children.text: Taking care of your health shouldn’t be a guessing game, but for many, navigating preventive care and understanding how insurance works feels overwhelming. Preventive care is designed to catch problems early—before they grow into something more serious or costly. Services like wellness visits, vaccinations, and screenings are vital for long-term health, and in many cases, insurance plans cover them completely.

Sometimes, though, insurance rules can make things tricky. Preventive visits—like your annual wellness exam—are generally covered at no cost to you. But there are certain factors, such as the time since your last visit, your specific insurance benefits, or your healthcare provider network status that can impact this coverage.
This isn’t unique to Tia; it’s how insurance companies define and process care.

At Tia, we’re here to bridge the gap between preventive care and the complex world of insurance, making it easier for you to access the care you need while keeping costs transparent and manageable. In this guide, we’ll explore what preventive care entails and how it’s covered by insurance. By the end, you’ll feel confident in planning your next visit, asking the right questions, and making informed decisions about your health.
What Is Preventive Care?

Preventive care is the foundation of a healthy lifestyle. It focuses on catching potential health issues early through proactive measures, helping you avoid serious conditions and costly treatments. Key components of preventive care include:
Screenings:
Tests for conditions like high blood pressure, cholesterol, diabetes, and certain cancers.
Vaccinations:
Immunizations to protect against infectious diseases, such as flu shots and childhood vaccines.
Wellness Visits:
Routine check-ups where your provider assesses your overall health, offers counseling, and discusses lifestyle factors.
The benefits of preventive care are clear—it improves outcomes, reduces the risk of chronic illnesses, and saves money by addressing issues early.
What’s Covered by Insurance?
Most in-network insurance plans cover many preventive services at 100%, including:
Annual well-woman exams
Cervical cancer screenings (pap smears)
Blood pressure, diabetes, and cholesterol tests
STI screenings
Immunizations

However, not all plans are created equal. For example, some may cover Vitamin D testing or nutritional counseling as preventive, while others do not. To avoid surprises, it’s important to confirm your coverage directly with your insurance provider.
Where Things Can Get Complicated…

The line between preventive and diagnostic care can be blurry. Preventive visits are routine check-ups aimed at maintaining health, while diagnostic visits focus on addressing specific symptoms or concerns. Just because you are receiving care for preventive purposes unfortunately doesn’t mean your insurance will consider the visit to be preventive. Understanding this distinction is crucial to avoid unexpected bills.
Diagnostic Scenario:
You schedule a preventive wellness visit, which is fully covered by your insurance.
During the visit, you mention experiencing frequent migraines.
Your provider evaluates the migraines, possibly prescribes medication, and orders tests.
In this case, the visit shifts from preventive to diagnostic, meaning you could be responsible for a copay or coinsurance, depending on your plan.
“Not Preventive” Scenario:
You schedule a preventive wellness visit, which is fully covered by your insurance.
During the visit, your provider states they want to order an ultrasound for your PCOS to confirm your current health status.
In this case, while the ultrasound was done for preventive reasons, insurance does not consider this service to be preventive based on criteria from the Affordable Care Act.
Timing Issue Scenario:
You schedule a preventive wellness visit for January 15, 2025, but your last preventive exam was February 28, 2024
Your visit is fully preventive with your regular annual physical checkup and screenings.

In this case, while your visit was truly preventive with no diagnostic issues and you only had “preventive” services completed, your insurance denied your appointment because it hadn’t been 365 days since your last annual preventive exam, resulting in a bill.
Actionable Tips for Maximizing Your Benefits
Before your next visit, take these steps to ensure a smooth experience:
Call Your Insurance Provider
: Ask what preventive services are covered, how often, and if there are any restrictions.
Prepare for Your Appointment
: Use Tia’s
Preventive Insurance Checklist
to guide your conversation with your insurer.
Understand Billing Scenarios
: Know what might trigger a shift from preventive to diagnostic care.
Preventive Insurance Checklist:
Does my insurance plan cover preventive care at 100%?
What preventive services are covered under my plan?
Are there limits on how often I can receive these services?
What specific screenings, vaccinations, or lab tests included?
What happens if a preventive visit becomes diagnostic?
Your Partner in Care

Preventive care is about more than avoiding illness—it’s an investment in your overall well-being. At Tia, we’re here to support you with personalized care, transparent costs, and guidance to help you navigate the complexities of insurance.
Take charge of your health today by scheduling your next preventive visit. If you have questions about your benefits or costs, reach out to Tia’s care team—we’re here to help every step of the way.
Ready to engage in preventive care? Join Tia and schedule an appointment to get started at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Stomach Pain & Issues

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for Sexual and Gynecological health
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
Seamlessly transition between virtual consults for birth control and in-person procedures like IUD insertion.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Your primary care provider is always in sync with your GYN care, so you never have to repeat your history to get a Pap test, STI screen, or tackle a more complex condition.
✓ Seamless Specialty Access
Tia partners with leading health systems to ensure that if you need specialty care—for complex issues like advanced endometriosis—you are referred and supported while Tia remains your home base.
✓ Holistic/comprehensive care
We use both medication and integrative options (like acupuncture or pelvic floor therapy referrals) for chronic conditions like PCOS or painful sex, aiming to get to the root and give you options.
✓ Care that grows with you
We are your health home for every stage—from your first Pap test to navigating menopause and beyond.

testimonials.Content: My experience at Tia was exceptional from start to finish. As someone with a 9–5 job, being able to book a 6 PM appointment for an OBGYN concern was a huge relief. From check-in, Angie made me feel welcomed and cared for, and Stephanie was professional, gentle with the blood draw, and made sure I felt comfortable the whole time. I saw Jamee, the nurse practitioner, who was incredibly supportive and knowledgeable. She took her time answering all my questions and helped me feel at ease with clear explanations and genuine care. Tia delivers warm, respectful, patient-focused care, and I’ve already started recommending it to friends and colleagues. Here, you truly feel heard and supported.
testimonials.Title: Clarissa B., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I had my IUD removed and replaced today which is probably one of the most uncomfortable procedures EVER but the care team made sure that my comfort was prioritized. In addition to the luxurious robe and beautiful interiors I've come to love from Tia, I was provided with a heating pad, white noise machine, and lavender essential oil to smell during my procedure. My NP was extremely thorough and asked if I had any questions and walked me through every step as it was happening. I felt very taken care of! As soon as I got home I was messaged aftercare information! It’s clear that Tia thinks through every step of their care and prioritizes comfort along with exceptional healthcare.
testimonials.Title: Chloe M., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: Amazing experience with Tia! The providers are compassionate and the office beautiful and efficient. The care is excellent with a focus on the whole person, offering mental health and hormone treatments as well as primary women's healthcare. Used acupuncture with my procedure and couldn't believe the difference it made. 10/10 recommend Tia to anyone
testimonials.Title: Alexis R.,Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I’ve read many reviews about this facility, but my experience was truly exceptional. I’ve had a lot of trauma around women’s health and avoided gynecologic care for years, but Tia completely changed that. Dr. Ryan Darvish is the first provider who made me feel genuinely safe and cared for. After an abnormal pap, I went through several difficult procedures, and he guided me through each one with compassion, clear communication, and a focus on keeping me comfortable. The same care and support were present during my IUD removal and replacement, which he made nearly painless. I’ve never felt so at ease during appointments that once felt overwhelming. Dr. Darvish is truly a gift, and I recommend him to anyone with fear or trauma around gynecologic care. Tia is now the only place I’ll go for my health needs.
testimonials.Title: Emily F., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I cannot say enough good things about my experience at Tia Clinic in Studio City, thanks to Jamee and Alauna. Their customer service was truly unmatched—every interaction was thoughtful, kind, and professional. Jamee was extremely patient with me, taking the time to answer every single one of my many questions without hesitation. She made me feel heard and valued, which is so rare these days. Both Jamee and Alauna went above and beyond to make me feel comfortable throughout my visit.  I’ve officially found my gyno for life with Jamee, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the care and support I received. If you’re looking for a team that genuinely cares about their patients, look no further than Tia Clinic.
testimonials.Title: Tia H., Tia Patient

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:
70% of Tia’s members hadn’t had a preventative exam in the year before joining Tia. After joining,
they complete life-saving screenings at rates 2-3x the national average.
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression, nearly
double the national benchmark
because we see mental health as a core part of your health.
It's healthcare that's proactive, not just reactive, designed to help you get well and stay well for the long term.

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

children.text: The Problem

Mental healthcare can feel fragmented, separating your mind from your body. When you feel overwhelmed, anxious, or like "something is off," you may be told it's "just stress" without a look at your hormones, sleep, digestion, or other causes. This leaves you managing symptoms without understanding
why
you feel this way, especially problematic for women who face disproportionately high rates of anxiety and depression compared to men.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right weight & metabolic health doctor and book your first visit.

Tia’s Solution for Women

Our mental health care starts with treating your body, not just your brain. Our expert-led whole-person approach looks at the full picture of your health—including the physiological effects of stress, hormones, and sleep—to find the root cause. You get a connected, relationship-based approach with an expanded toolkit, including care from specialty trained primary care providers and psychiatric specialists if needed. We consider mental health a core part of your physical health, ensuring consistent, holistic, and long-term support.

Why Mental Health matters for women
The Connection is Read: Your Mind
is
Your Body

You deserve to feel like yourself again. The belief that mental health and physical health are separate has led to a fragmented system where patients are often left to piece together care on their own. This is frustrating, especially since the impact of mental health on chronic conditions like heart disease, diabetes, and stroke is significant and preventable. Our model is designed to support you by acknowledging that your mental, physical, and reproductive health are inextricably linked.
When “Just Stressed” Gets Overlooked
Women experience anxiety and depression at nearly
twice

the rate of men, yet stress and mood changes are often hard to fully assess in today’s fragmented healthcare system. Key contributors, like hormones, sleep, pain, and life context, can go unrecognized, especially when national depression screening rates in primary care hover around 39%.
Our integrated approach is designed to make it easier to catch what’s really going on. That’s why over
of our patients receive evidence-based depression screening.
A Model Build for the Long Term

Mental health care shouldn't be limited to just one solution. Tia's integrated model is designed to empower you with personalized care, offering a full suite of options from clinical to subclinical modalities—like medication, acupuncture, or supplements. By connecting your primary care with specialized psychiatric support when needed, we ensure you have a collaborative Care Team providing a personalized path toward feeling like your whole self again.e.

“We're here to support our patients in making the decisions that are right for them. Whatever you’re going through, I want you to know: Tia is here to support you feeling better—physically and emotionally. Because improved mental health is improved health.”
Jessica Horwitz, Chief Clinical Officer
How Tia is different for Mental Health
Tia's approach treats your mental well-being as a core part of your physical health, not something separate.
✓ We Connect Mind, Body, and Hormones

Our providers are specialty trained to look at the whole-system effects of stress across your sleep, digestion, hormones, and more. To ensure we get to the root of your experience, together we may consider additional labs and testing for your unique situation.
✓ An Expanded, Expert-Led Toolkit

You get comprehensive care from specialty trained primary care providers, backed by psychiatric mental health nurse practitioners as needed. Your plan offers a wide range of solutions beyond medication—from acupuncture and supplements to holistic planning and nutrition counseling.
✓ Support Designed for Women's Life Stages

We understand that anxiety and depression rates are disproportionately high for women and often surface during major life transitions. We offer care for women navigating hormonal shifts, postpartum recovery, and perimenopause, providing consistent follow-up and long-term support.

modules.title: Book a Weight & Metabolic Health Visit
Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Feeling confused or judged about your experience with weight and metabolism is a common but frustrating experience. In fact, ~60% of patients report experiencing bias from doctors. When care focuses only on the scale or offers quick fixes, it often fails to look at the full picture of your physical, emotional, and metabolic health. With the rise of prescription solutions like GLP-1s, many women are also left wondering what is right for their body and how to navigate side effects or long-term management. This fragmented approach leaves you feeling stuck and like you've tried everything.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right weight & metabolic health doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution
Tia's Weight & Metabolic Health care is built to understand your whole body, not just a number. Our providers are trained on a Health at Every Size (HAES) approach , meaning the goal is helping
you
feel well, not fit someone else's idea of wellness. We create a personalized, non-judgmental Care Plan, integrating all factors from hormones and sleep to stress and nutrition for long-term support.

Experiencing unexplained weight gain or weight loss
Feeling stuck in current routines and like you’ve “tried everything” to manage your weight
Curious about or seeking GLP-1s, other weight management medications, or non-rx weight management solutions
Needing help managing chronic conditions like diabetes or high blood pressure.
Wanting a non-judgmental plan focused on long-term wellness and feeling well.
Seeking evaluation for associated issues like high cholesterol or sleep concerns.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Weight & Metabolic Health matters for women
Metabolic Health is More Than a Number

Weight and metabolism are complex, often emotionally-charged subjects. Because metabolic health doesn't happen in a vacuum, our providers look at the bigger picture—from family history to hormones—to create a holistic path forward. We reject the idea that issues are caused by poor self-image or self-control. You deserve compassionate, individualized support that validates your concerns and takes into account your whole health.
Your Biology Wasn't Made for Today's Stress

Human metabolism was made to hold on to fat. When we're stressed, we crave sugar—a biology that is maladaptive for the chronic stresses of modern life. When women seek solutions, we know it's not because they did anything "wrong," but because of what's happened in their lives and in the world. Furthermore, it's normal for chronic stress and a history of trauma to increase insulin resistance and weight gain.
Your Care Should Connect the Dots

Metabolic challenges are present throughout life, but they become particularly critical during life transitions like menopause, where shifts in visceral fat lead to increased insulin resistance and cardiovascular risks. Conditions like metabolic syndrome, PCOS, diabetes, and hypertension shouldn’t be separated into the silos of endocrine, cardiac, and/or gynecologic care. Helping patients heal requires caring for those interactions within the context of the whole person and their life.

“When women come to Tia seeking weight support or metabolic health solutions, we know it’s not because they did anything “wrong,” but because of what’s happened in their lives (and in our world). It’s natural to need more support when your metabolism changes during menopause and other life transitions.”
Emily Lu, MD
, Medical Director Quality & Primary Care
How Tia is different for Weight & Metabolic Health

At Tia, we know that your weight and metabolism don't work in isolation—they are connected to your hormones, stress, and lifestyle. We don't focus only on the scale or offer a one-size-fits-all quick fix. Instead, we look beyond single solutions to understand how metabolic health connects with your mental, physical, and emotional well-being.
✓ Non-Judgmental Partnership

We approach weight and metabolism with compassionate, individualized support. We focus on your goals and utilize a Health at Every Size (HAES) approach, meaning your wellness is our priority, not fitting someone else's idea of a "healthy" body.
✓ Whole-Person Assessment

Our team takes time to listen, investigate root causes, and connect the dots across your health—including sleep, hormones, and mental health—to create a comprehensive care plan that reflects your goals and lifestyle.
✓ Expanded Toolkit & Evidence-Based Care

We offer options for both medication (including GLP-1s) and non-medication treatments, such as referrals to dedicated nutrition partners, medication management, and emotional support. Our care is evidence-based and personalized, not prescriptive.
✓ Long-Term Support

We're not here for a one-time prescription. Tia’s model is built for ongoing continuity of care through dedicated follow-up visits to track progress, adjust your plan, and ensure you feel supported over time.

children.text: Consider acupuncture if you're:
Looking to support your hormones or hormone-related issues including PCOS, menopause, perimenopause, or cramping
Seeking relief from stress and/or support for anxiety or mood imbalances
Exploring alternatives for pain management for migraines, chronic pain, muscle soreness, or after a procedure like IUD insertion
Trying to conceive or seeking support on your pregnancy journey
Trust the science, the benefits are real
More than 2 out of 3 Tia members see improvement in their symptoms*

The intent of acupuncture is to bring the body back into a state of homeostasis and ignite the body’s innate ability to heal–and that can take some time. It often takes up to 6 treatments within a 3 month window to see significant results.
*Based on self reported data from N=120 Tia acupuncture patients who have had 6 or more appointments in the last 3 months.
What to expect when you try Acupuncture at Tia
The Environment
Relax and let everything melt away. Acupuncture at Tia is done in a semi-private room surrounded by curtains and supported by calming sounds and mood lighting.
The Appointment

Your 60-minute session is divided into two parts: You'll spend the first 20-minutes with your provider discussing your symptoms, goals and progress. Once you’ve aligned, your provider will get into the treatment. You'll spend the remaining 40-minutes in a deep state of relaxation, while the needles do their work.
Check our price list.
Find Acupuncture Services Near You
Meet our Acupuncturists

When you come to Tia for acupuncture, your acupuncturist will work in collaboration with your primary care providers, OB/GYN, and therapist. From two-part appointments like IUD insertion and acupuncture to offering holistic support for fertility, we believe in connected care that treats the whole you.
Why our members love Tia

testimonials.Content: I had to undergo a procedure here, and I was initially terrified about the pain involved. Fortunately, Nurse Practitioners Julianne and Alex, along with Acupuncturist Erica, provided incredible support throughout the entire process. Their compassionate care, from medication for pain and anxiety relief to soothing acupuncture, made me feel safe and supported in the room. I truly appreciate the way they held my hand and guided me through what could have been a very difficult experience. It's incredible to feel safe with other women healthcare professionals. Thank you, team, for making this so much easier for me
testimonials.Title: Taylor L.

testimonials.Content: Tia has been an incredible resource for my health as a woman. It has been such a relief to work with a team of health care practitioners who understand women’s health and are committed to addressing things at the root cause. The combination of western and eastern medical practices has been life changing and it gives me peace of mind to have the majority of my healthcare needs addressed under one umbrella. Their team is so responsive and consistent and supportive. I specifically would like to recommend Paolo Chico at the Silver Lake location for acupuncture.
testimonials.Title: Hannah R.

testimonials.Content: I love the Tia Clinic!! Been going there mostly for group acupuncture and have consistently had an amazing experience. Group acupuncture appointments are super affordable—that alone has made my annual membership basically pay for itself. The space is unbelievably beautiful and well thought out, and I love the acupuncturist, Elana.

children.text: See specific benefits by service:
Important Disclaimer:

Tia does not accept HMO plans or Medicare/Medicaid. Tia’s care model is built around integrated primary care, mental health, and gynecology. Many HMO and Medicare/Medicaid plans are not structured to reimburse this type of whole person care, which is why we are not able to accept these plan types today.
Don’t have insurance? See our Cash Price List
Predictable Payments
We know that navigating healthcare can be difficult. That's why Tia is committed to access for all and offers Predictable Payment options to remove financial stress.
What is Predictable Payments?

Predictable Payments is our new capped invoicing system that ensures that no matter the total amount you owe, you will only ever be billed $100 per month until your balance is paid off. Depending on the care you receive, this could look like: the total amount owed for care might be $300, but the total due this month is $100. This method offers a clear, manageable way to handle medical expenses, allowing you to focus on what truly matters—your health.
Benefits of Predictable Payments:
1. $100 Capped Invoices

One of the biggest benefits of this payment method is predictability. With a consistent $100 cap, you can budget with confidence and avoid fluctuating or surprising bills. We know that some months you may need more time to pay, and that’s ok. Our goal is to remove financial stress so you never delay the care you need.
2. One Invoice, One Time a Month

The healthcare billing process can be complicated and confusing. Our new method simplifies this by providing clear, straightforward billing. You’ll receive a single, predictable invoice each month, making it easier to understand and manage your healthcare expenses.
3. Surprise-Free = Stress-Free

Medical expenses can be overwhelming, especially when they come as a surprise. Our capped invoicing system alleviates this stress by spreading out payments into smaller, more manageable amounts. This approach helps prevent financial strain and ensures you can maintain financial stability.
4. Decision-Making For Your Health, Not Your Wallet

We believe in empowering our members with the tools and resources they need to make informed decisions about their health. Capped invoicing is part of our commitment to transparency and member-centric care. We aim to partner with you not just around the care you receive but also how you budget for the costs associated with the care you need and deserve.
Want to know more about Predictable Payments?
Talk to Our Team
Have questions about Tia’s care?
Speak with a member of our Engagement Team at

children.text: About Tia San Francisco

Located conveniently at the intersection of Mission and Van Ness Ave. Designed to make the female healthcare experience more welcoming and comforting, Tia members can enjoy an open layout waiting room with specialty beverages and new books to browse through.

cells: Our address is 1500 Mission Street. Enter the front door of the building on Mission St, below the Tia sign. Ring the doorbell to your left.

SF Mission offers parking validation for the parking garage at 90 11th Street, San Francisco CA. The price starts at $10/hour but should not exceed $20 total with parking validation from the clinic. If garage has sign stating full, please disregard and proceed to park there as we have reserved spot for Tia patients.
When to arrive to the clinic before your appointment

Please arrive to your appointment 15 min early, in case you get lost or have trouble with traffic or parking. If you are late to your appointment, we may not be able to care for you and you may be charged a fee.

children.text: Your Tia membership includes access to a Care Coordinator, virtual and in-person appointments, community programming, and more. Membership does not include the cost of appointments, but as a member you can use your insurance for gynecology, primary care, and, depending on your insurance, acupuncture too!

faqs.question: Does membership include the cost of appointments?

children.text:
Yes, through our annual Whole Health Exam!

We fuse your gynecology and primary care check-ups (& more!) into a singular, cohesive experience that’s personalized, actionable, and convenient. We intentionally separate your virtual health assessment from your in-person exams and services (delivered at the clinic) so that you get more 1:1 time with your provider, plus additional opportunities for check-ins throughout the year to evaluate and tweak your Care Plan.

As long as you have not had an annual exam or gynecological exam in the past year (we do it all in your Whole Health Exam!) your in-clinic part 2 should be 100% covered by in-network insurance. We accept in-network insurance for part 1 and 3 but you may be responsible for a copay or coinsurance depending on your plan. We always recommend checking with your health plan before booking.
Read more about Tia’s annual Whole Health Exam.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:
70% of Tia’s members hadn’t had a preventative exam in the year before joining Tia. After joining,
they complete life-saving screenings at rates 2-3x the national average.
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression, nearly
double the national benchmark
because we see mental health as a core part of your health.
It's healthcare that's proactive, not just reactive, designed to help you get well and stay well for the long term.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

Tia’s primary care services may be right for you if you:
Need a primary care provider or are overdue for an annual exam
Have a common illness like a cough, cold, flu, or a UTI.
Experiencing headaches, body aches, or unexplained pain.
Need support managing a chronic condition like high blood pressure or diabetes.
Feel anxious, overwhelmed, or "off," and you're not sure why.
Need a prescription refilled or want to discuss medication management.
Have ongoing issues like gut problems or trouble sleeping.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary gynecologist and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:
Integrated Prevention:
After joining Tia, members complete
life-saving screenings at rates 2–3x the national average
, ensuring your gynecologic health (like Pap tests and cervical cancer screenings) is proactively managed.
Seamless Specialty Referrals:
If you need specialty care, Tia partners with top health systems to coordinate your referral while remaining your primary care home.
Mental Health is GYN Health
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression
because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.
